page: Microsoft Azure D96alds_v7 is a compute-intensive virtual machine from the Daldsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 48 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor using the x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration includes 192.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core. For local storage, the instance is equipped with 5669 GB of local disk capacity and is premium storage capable. The CPU supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and cryptographic acceleration via AES, VAES, and SHA_NI.

In benchmark evaluations, the D96alds_v7 demonstrates top-tier performance, placing in the top 10% for both single-core and multi-core stress-ng CPU tests. Its memory subsystem achieves strong results, with read-only large block bandwidth in the top 25% and write large block bandwidth in the top 10%. Computational efficiency is further highlighted by top-tier scores in PassMark CPU tests, including extended instructions, encryption, and physics simulations. Database and key-value operations are highly performant, with Redis SET operations and PassMark database operations both ranking in the top 10%. Additionally, the server displays strong to top-tier capabilities in LLM inference speeds across small, medium, and large models.

The primary tradeoff of the D96alds_v7 is its low memory density of 2.0 GB per vCPU, which limits its suitability for memory-bound applications despite its massive compute capacity. However, this configuration offers high cost efficiency for compute-heavy workloads that do not require large memory footprints, as users do not pay for idle RAM. The inclusion of 5669 GB of local storage provides high-speed data access, making this server well-suited for high-throughput database operations, static web serving, parallelized CPU-bound tasks, data compression, and localized LLM inference.
description: Microsoft Azure D96alds_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with 96 dedicated vCPUs and 192.0 GB of RAM. This configuration yields a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU, paired with a substantial 5669 GB of local storage. Operating on the x86_64 architecture under Microsoft Hyper-V, the server supports AVX-512 and cryptographic extensions. Benchmarks place the D96alds_v7 in the top 10% for multi-core CPU performance, database operations, and Redis SET throughput. While the low memory density presents a tradeoff for memory-intensive applications, it provides high resource efficiency for compute-heavy tasks. This server is optimized for high-throughput database workloads, static web serving, data compression, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D96alds_v7 is a compute-optimized server with 96 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 5669 GB local storage, delivering top-tier multi-core performance and database throughput.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D96alds_v7 features 96 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 5669 GB local storage, optimized for high-performance compute workloads.
tagline: A high-compute, low-memory virtual server featuring ninety-six AMD EPYC vCPUs and massive local storage for intensive parallel processing.
bullet_points: Powered by 96 dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 vCPUs and 192.0 GB of RAM.
Equipped with 5669 GB of local storage and premium storage capabilities.
Top-tier CPU performance in both single-core and multi-core benchmarks.
Supports AVX-512 and cryptographic hardware acceleration.
Low memory-to-core ratio optimized for compute-heavy, non-RAM-bound tasks.
Excellent fit for high-throughput databases, web serving, and LLM inference.
